sectionsUnavailable: Authenticated Sequencing.com sample report rows are not available yet.
No calibrated athletic-performance model is supplied with this fixture.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived ACTN3 rs1815739 CT call is available. This is trait context only and does not predict athletic ability.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: ACTN3 is one of the best-studied genes in athletic-performance research, but performance is shaped by many genetic and non-genetic factors.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived ACE ID context row is available. This is not enough to choose sports, predict talent, or prescribe training.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: ACE is discussed in athletic-performance research, but environment, training, and many other factors remain important.

trainingContext: Use the report as educational context, not as a training plan.
Discuss training, injury prevention, and performance goals with qualified coaches or health professionals.
Do not use genotype alone for sport selection or expectations about athletic ability.

references.title: NHGRI overview of polygenic risk scores

missingInputs: Authenticated sample-report sections and rows have not been extracted.
No calibrated athletic-performance probability model is supplied.
Most known and unknown genetic contributors to athletic performance are not represented in this fixture.
uncertainty: Athletic performance is a complex trait influenced by genetics, training, environment, opportunity, and many other factors.
The significance of many performance-associated variants is limited or uncertain.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.
The report is educational and does not predict elite ability, prescribe training, or recommend sport selection.
A single or small number of markers cannot explain athletic performance.
